recentTweets.text:
A year ago today, I gave one of the biggest speeches of my life from Wyandotte, Michigan. 

Let’s review the bidding. https://t.co/OGEviUOvsR

In 2020, President Trump told us nine months in advance that if he lost, then the election had to be rigged.

We had a failure of imagination then – and it ended with a violent mob on January 6th. 

Never again. https://t.co/sZDtRclD9i

Important results tonight on Prop 50 passing in California. In a perfect world, federal law would prohibit the re-drawing of districts to be for one party or another. But President Trump pushed several states to redraw districts to maintain his power, so CA is fighting fire with

Tonight, my good friend @SpanbergerForVA has made history. Just like in 2018, she flipped a Republican seat—and this time, as the first female governor in VA history. Abigail’s background in national security and her ruthless focus on the cost of living shone through in her https://t.co/BJW1G19O6P

Ban the presidential pardon.

https://t.co/ibzXT0PnSR https://t.co/aeCpoHK5Mf

Each of us swore an oath to the Constitution. We are all watching what President Trump is doing with the uniformed military. 

As people who have served, it’s important to speak out.

So on 10/18 let's do what we do best.

Wear the red, white and blue.

Wave the flag. Peacefully. https://t.co/NFVoqbHfwi

Leaking confidential info to politically weaponize @MikieSherrill’s  long record of service to her country is below the belt, and publishing her personal info should be completely off-limits.

No matter your political persuasion, most Americans are sick of this nasty, zero-sum

We cannot become numb to this type of corruption. This is a senior official in charge of stewarding taxpayer dollars. We need access to this FBI case file, and then review the taxpayer dollars that Tom Homan has overseen at the White House. https://t.co/MJLIrPZZru
President Trump is killing new energy projects because he thinks they’re “woke.” He’s making this ridiculous political association when we as Americans just need more energy. https://t.co/xwc2wj0p7y
President Trump campaigned on lowering our costs on day one. But today, in every single category, he is raising our costs. All of us need to keep book on that. https://t.co/thiaIMdlze

No party is “entitled” to any seat in Congress. Those seats belong to the people. Politicians shouldn’t get to choose their voters, and the President should stop trying to rig the game because he’s scared he might lose in 2026. https://t.co/AhbHQ4u0ZQ

The debate is no longer progressive vs. moderate; that is very 2017. 

The debate is how do you answer one question: Is the second Trump administration an existential threat to democracy or is it bad but survivable?

I believe it is an existential threat. https://t.co/0AT2AUjMqo

I did a little middle-class math on President Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill. 

It’s not good. 

Tax benefits for the wealthiest. Higher health care costs for everyone else. 

You better believe this is the same health care fight from Trump’s first term. https://t.co/CFx8S1wibx
https://t.co/bWLCAPH7LN

Last night, Senators-elect were taken to the National Archives to go see the vaults — and some of the most sacred documents of our country’s history. As a history nerd, this was a true highlight.  

We were shown the original oath of office, signed by General George Washington, https://t.co/ON533U5JHW

Feeling like the spirit of the great Michigan Senator, Phil Hart, is looking down on me as we move in to our temporary office on the 8th floor of the Hart Building. 

Not only is it great to be in the building honoring "the conscience of the Senate,” but on top of that, I'm the https://t.co/bQsBXP41zx

Six Senate Dems in the freshman class.  We’ve gotten a tour of the Senate floor and cloakrooms, taken ID pictures, gotten briefed on employment rules and laws, and had a lunch with sitting members on starting up an office. https://t.co/ml1MiheWOd
Senate orientation has officially begun! https://t.co/yReo7js2rv

Interested in working in our brand new Senate office? We’re hiring!  We need conscientious, hardworking folks who are ready to serve the people of Michigan. 

We have job openings in both Michigan and Washington D.C. that will start after January 3rd, 2025. Take a look at our job
The best campaign team in the business! Thanks to my incredible team for all the work in making this win possible. https://t.co/mjGScrTUKP
First piece of mail: A FedEx from the Senate. Senate orientation starts Tuesday, and I got my book today! https://t.co/BKsVAKb3MW

THANK YOU, MICHIGAN! I am so honored to be the next Senator from the great state of Michigan and to follow in the footsteps of the great @Stabenow. 

This would not have happened without the hard work and support of so many: my family, our volunteers, donors, and of course — https://t.co/d8Mj8C9qRq

The polls are now closed across Michigan! It has been a true honor to be on the ballot to be your next U.S. Senator. I’m so grateful to everyone across the state who helped make this campaign happen, whether you chipped in a few bucks, knocked some doors, or talked to your
Just voted here in Holly alongside my family. It’s going to be a good day, Michigan! Get out and vote! https://t.co/8o7zp1ZHoj

I fought like hell to pass the CHIPS Act, and it’s already creating good paying jobs and bringing manufacturing back to Michigan. Repealing it will only hurt workers and roll back the progress we’ve made to bring supply chains back home. It’s not just bad for Michigan, it’s

.@Liz_Cheney is right: In this election, all eyes are on Michigan. And a week from tomorrow, we have the opportunity to send a clear message about the type of state, and country, we want to be. https://t.co/NLJ2uUBqNg

Hey Michigan — early voting starts statewide TODAY! Michigan is a swingy state and that means *every* vote counts — but don’t just take my word for it, listen to what a few friends of mine had to say. 

Thank you to the West Wing Cast: @BradleyWhitford, Martin Sheen, https://t.co/V2As4Wyd6f

THANK YOU to my former boss, President @BarackObama and the one and only @Eminem for helping fire up voters in Detroit last night. 

It was truly a full circle moment for me: as a CIA officer, I was detailed to work in the Obama White House, so it was amazing to be on stage with https://t.co/SKGzo6HlZu
I’m pretty sure I just lived out every Michigander’s dream — hard to imagine anything cooler than sharing a stage with @Eminem. https://t.co/6xxF2shJoO
In Detroit with @KamalaHarris, @garlin, and @MayorMikeDuggan with 17 days left to go! https://t.co/Zc8otSYWB6
I mean, come on Michigan — now you’re just showing off. https://t.co/DZGvMo1sv5
Gun violence is the number one killer of children under 21 in our country. You're either willing to do something about it, or you aren't. @MikeRogersforMI isn't. https://t.co/wvnsSB3oWq
Betsy DeVos and her entire family have contributed to @MikeRogersforMI for years -- it makes sense he adopts her approach of demonizing public schools and teachers. https://t.co/Tjous0Dmdi
Past behavior is the best indicator of future behavior. There are no "rebrands" when you have a 20-year record of fighting to end a woman's right to choose. https://t.co/wPm9lHznWu
Detroit vs. Everybody

For the Republicans out there who feel that their party has left them: you will always have an open door to my office. 

If you’re looking for someone who cares about getting something done, who will listen, who will always have a seat at the table for you, no matter your https://t.co/MwBAiAxWPK

We know @MikeRogersforMI’s record. He voted to raise the retirement age, he voted to privatize or cut Social Security, and voted against allowing Medicare to negotiate drug prices. Maybe he forgot that when he was down in Florida. #MISen https://t.co/mGocAaey5p

.@MikeRogersforMI had 20 years as a legislator. He voted for every single ban, every single restriction, every single bill that came across his desk.

Every single time he was cast those votes, he sent a message to women: that he doesn’t trust you to make your own decisions about https://t.co/0LmuCkRIiJ
Reproductive freedom is not a talking point. It's our lives. https://t.co/GVGLKkTju2
I don’t have to hide from my record on abortion: I believe a woman has the right to decide when and how she has a family and I will vote to restore Roe in the Senate. #MISen

.@MikeRogersforMI and I both have voting records on Medicare and Social Security. I believe in supporting it. Mike voted to privatize or cut it over and over again. 

It’s all in the record. Just check the facts. #MISen https://t.co/Vy88eM7QF8

I’m so grateful to my former boss, President @BarackObama, for his support of my campaign. He was one of two presidents I served in the White House, and I hope to bring the same brand of independent and effective public service he talks about in this ad to the Senate. 

Watch https://t.co/OTKi4kY3rM
Welcome to Michigan, @corybooker! https://t.co/1Udb9oT2jM

See @JDVance, it’s not that hard to buy a donut and, ya know, make pleasant conversation. Thanks to Strange Matter Coffee for serving up great coffee and great fun, one donut at a time. https://t.co/3UEQr1IdgS
When someone shows you who they are, believe them. https://t.co/f9vsd7c0pD
.@jonstewart, welcome to the SlotHive 🌭 https://t.co/oz8uxbWdsq
We are the damn United States of America. We do not follow — we lead. https://t.co/5KRd92skyJ
Am I wrong?

THANK YOU, MICHIGAN!!

Thank you to everyone who cast their ballot in this primary election – and to everyone who knocked a door, talked to voters, or chipped in a few bucks to fuel this campaign. I’m thrilled to officially be your Democratic Nominee for Senate. And for those who
Donald Trump just tore into me at his event here in Michigan. He knows our campaign is the only thing keeping Republicans from flipping the Senate. https://t.co/aAyDbywULu

But Donald Trump isn't coming to Michigan next week out of concern for her — he's coming to turn her death into a political performance for his own benefit. For all his talk about the border, Trump and his allies *blocked* a bipartisan https://t.co/b88ygVJ7y7

Come on, Mike.

In your 14 years in Congress, you co-sponsored FOUR bills — the last one with Jim Jordan in 2013 — that would have the same effect as the Alabama Supreme Court’s IVF ruling, making it impossible for millions of couples to have a family. You don’t get to run away

Donald Trump *just* got off the stage in Michigan. It was his 3rd time here in 8 months. He knows he can’t win the White House — and Republicans can’t flip the Senate — without winning Michigan. https://t.co/1bvAmdrS5Z

Ooooohhhhiiiiiooooooo!!! Midwest is showing UP tonight!!!

Congrats, Ohio — and welcome to the family of states where voters have protected reproductive freedom!

Actually, at that moment I was on the phone with the State Department trying to get American citizens out of Israel and Gaza. Meanwhile, all the Republicans have done is fight each other because they can’t elect a speaker. Get your house in order so we can all get to work!

I’ve been asked a lot this week, why do you want to run for Senate? For me, it’s all about service: After 9/11, I was recruited to the CIA & served three tours in Iraq alongside the U.S. military. I later served under Presidents Bush and Obama at the White House & then at the

Mr. President, my name is Elissa Slotkin. I served 3 tours in Iraq w/ CIA, served both GOP+Dem admins and believe in putting my country before anything else. In Congress, I'll be accountable to Michigan voters -- not Leader Pelosi, not corporate special interests, or anyone else.

In Michigan, we believe in the fundamental rights of all people, even when we disagree. We’re not threatened by books or the free exchange of ideas. And as you may have noticed last November, we reject the politics of division and hate. Sell that junk somewhere else.
I’d challenge any of my Republican colleagues to explain to a 17-year-old who’s been impacted by gun violence what they’re doing to keep our kids safe. The answer is nothing. https://t.co/d5ATanhWNY

Comparing gun safety measures to the mass extermination of 6 million people is hateful & ignorant, and it comes from party leaders who are out of ideas & catering to the fringe of the fringe. Delete it, apologize, and figure out what kind of party you want to be.

I’ve been asked a lot this week, why do you want to run for Senate? For me, it’s all about service: After 9/11, I was recruited to the CIA & served three tours in Iraq alongside the U.S. military. I later served under Presidents Bush and Obama at the White House & then at the

Today, I’m announcing my run to be Michigan’s next U.S. Senator.

We need a new generation of leaders that thinks differently, works harder, and never forgets that we are *public servants* https://t.co/L8cLgEUwnA

5/ There is no need to choose between having a gun for sport and caring about protecting our kids. We can do both. But folks in DC think it’s heresy to even come to the table to discuss how to keep our kids safe from gun violence. They don’t get it.

4/ But this is what I think politicians in Washington are totally missing. They’re the last ones to get the memo. The country is changing, while our elected leaders are still scared of the vocal minority who refuse to talk about any gun safety changes whatsoever.

2/ These folks call to tell me that while they love their hobby & don’t want someone taking their guns, they also can’t stand to see the gun violence we’re seeing again and again and again. These folks are just as fed up and angry as anyone who’s been calling me.

3/ They are parents and grandparents who are watching our children change because of gun violence, and they want something done. I started to see this after Oxford, it deepened with Uvalde, and now it’s ramped up even more after the shooting at MSU.

This is how I know the ground is shifting in Michigan on the issue of gun safety: Since Monday, I’ve received more messages of support for MSU than I can count, including a ton of texts & calls from Republican constituents & leaders who I know to be avid hunters & sportsmen. 1/
